summary: Quality assessment and comprehensive test strategy for venue search fuzzy matching improvement. Task requires handling typos, partial names, and word order variations while maintaining 300ms debounce performance. Critical focus areas: similarity threshold validation (0.80), input validation enforcement (3-100 chars), error resilience across 3 data sources, and 80%+ test coverage.

requirements: Query parameter validation: min 3 chars (DoS prevention), max 100 chars (size limit), reject if null/empty
All database queries use Prisma parameterized statements (already compliant)
Rate limiting enforced: 30 requests per minute per IP (already implemented in route.ts, must test)
No PII logging: search queries are safe (venue names are public), but never log user IDs or coordinates in search functions
No unhandled exceptions reach client as 500 with stack trace (catch all in route handler, return generic error message)
External calls (DB queries, Ticketmaster API, Photon API) have explicit error handling with timeouts (3s DB, 3s TM, 5s Photon)
Timeout fallback must not crash: returns partial results from successful sources or empty array
Custom error response for rate limiting (429 Retry-After header)
Database timeouts logged as WARNING with source name; errors logged as ERROR with operation context
Structured logging: log query length, number of venues processed, final result count, similarity threshold decisions (DEBUG level)
Never log: venue names, user IDs, coordinates, search queries verbatim (generic operation logging only)
Sentry integration: all errors in /api/venues/search must be captured (already in route.ts, test this)
No N+1 query patterns: venue search must use single aggregation query (already compliant with raw SQL)
Database indexes verified: trigram index exists on Concert.venue (confirmed in migration 20260326000000)
Levenshtein distance calculation must complete in < 20ms for 100 venues; total orchestration < 200ms with 3s source timeouts
All new functions have unit tests: searchDatabaseVenues(), calculateSimilarity(), normalizeForVenueComparison()
Integration tests for orchestration function searchVenuesEnhanced() covering 3-source timeout scenarios
API route tests covering input validation, rate limiting, error handling (15+ test cases minimum)
Coverage target: 80% line + branch coverage on venues.ts and route.ts; no untested error paths
Function naming: camelCase verbs (calculateSimilarity, normalizeForVenueComparison), constants UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
JSDoc on all public functions: @param {type} name, @returns {type} description, @throws error types
Complex business logic (Levenshtein algorithm, similarity threshold logic) documented with WHY comments explaining threshold choice
Migration includes trigram index creation (confirmed); verify index is active: SELECT * FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename = 'concert'
Future optimization: levenshtein() PostgreSQL function for venue counts > 10k (document in code comment, not required for this PR)

critical_requirements: [C] Input validation: enforce 3 char min, 100 char max; reject null/empty queries before any DB calls
[C] Error handling: all external calls (DB, API) wrapped in try/catch; timeouts must not crash application
[C] Security: no stack traces in error responses; rate limiting enforced (already implemented, must test)
[C] No PII logging: search queries and user IDs never logged
[C] Timeout resilience: one source timeout must not break the orchestration (return partial results or empty array)
high_requirements: [H] Test coverage: 80%+ line coverage; 15+ test cases covering happy path, edge cases, error scenarios
[H] Performance: total search orchestration < 200ms with 3s source timeouts (Levenshtein < 20ms for typical query)
[H] Similarity threshold: 0.80 (80% match); test boundary cases (0.79 must NOT match, 0.80 must match)
[H] Documentation: JSDoc on all public functions with @throws for error paths; complex logic explained
[H] Fuzzy matching algorithm: Levenshtein distance with normalization (lowercase, punctuation removal, diacritics)

setup.coverage_target: 80% line + branch coverage on venues.ts and route.ts

performance_benchmarks.notes: O(n × m²) where n=100, m=avg query length ~10; should be acceptable

naming_requirements.convention: camelCase verb + subject; makes purpose clear
camelCase verb + descriptor; indicates it's for venue-specific comparison
UPPER_SNAKE_CASE; indicates it's a configuration constant

items.requirement: Update JSDoc to mention fuzzy matching
items.template:
/**
 * Search venues from database with fuzzy matching and user-weighted scoring.
 *
 * Uses Levenshtein distance to find venues with >= 80% similarity to the query.
 * Results scored by user visit count (if authenticated) and global frequency.
 *
 * @param query - Search query string (3-100 characters)
 * @param userId - Optional user ID for personalized scoring
 * @param limit - Maximum results (default 10)
 * @returns Array of venue results with scores, sorted by relevance
 * @throws Error if query validation fails (checked before this is called)
 */

/**
 * Calculate similarity between two strings using Levenshtein distance.
 *
 * Strings are normalized (lowercase, punctuation removed) before comparison.
 * Similarity = 1 - (distance / maxLength), where distance is edit distance.
 *
 * @param name1 - First string to compare
 * @param name2 - Second string to compare
 * @returns Similarity score from 0 (no match) to 1 (exact match)
 * @throws TypeError if name1 or name2 is not a string
 */

/**
 * Normalize a string for venue name comparison.
 *
 * Applies the following transformations:
 * - Convert to lowercase
 * - Remove diacritics (ü -> u, é -> e) using Unicode NFKD
 * - Remove punctuation (hyphens, dots, apostrophes, commas)
 * - Trim leading/trailing whitespace
 *
 * Used for fuzzy matching; does not affect display names.
 *
 * @param name - Venue name to normalize
 * @returns Normalized string suitable for Levenshtein comparison
 */

items.requirement: Update route docstring to mention fuzzy matching and threshold
items.template:
/**
 * GET /api/venues/search
 * Search for event venues with fuzzy matching across multiple sources.
 *
 * Sources (in priority order):
 * 1. Database - User's concert history with fuzzy matching (80% similarity threshold)
 * 2. Ticketmaster - Concert-specific venue database
 * 3. Photon/OpenStreetMap - General geocoding fallback
 *
 * Fuzzy matching handles typos, partial names, and punctuation variations.
 * E.g., 'hanns-martin-schleyer-ha' matches 'Hanns-Martin-Schleyer-Halle'.
 *
 * Query parameters:
 * - q: Search query (required, 3-100 characters)
 * - lat: Latitude for biasing results (optional, -90 to 90)
 * - lon: Longitude for biasing results (optional, -180 to 180)
 *
 * Returns: Array of EnhancedVenueResult objects, sorted by relevance score
 *
 * Personalization fields (isUserVenue, userVisitCount) are only included
 * for authenticated users viewing their own venue history.
 */

security_requirements.requirement: Enforce 3-100 character length; reject null/empty; validate before any DB calls
security_requirements.implementation: Add validation at start of GET handler and searchDatabaseVenues(); return 200 with empty array if invalid

security_requirements.requirement: All queries parameterized via Prisma; no string concatenation in raw queries
security_requirements.implementation: Verify: existing raw query in searchDatabaseVenues uses Prisma.$queryRaw with interpolation (already safe); no changes needed

security_requirements.requirement: 30 requests per minute per IP; return 429 with Retry-After header
security_requirements.implementation: Already implemented in route.ts; must test 31st request triggers 429

security_requirements.requirement: Never log search queries, user IDs, coordinates; generic operation logging only
security_requirements.implementation: Log structure: 'venue.search', query_length, result_count, timeout_source (if applicable)

security_requirements.requirement: No stack traces or internal details in error responses
security_requirements.implementation: Catch all errors in GET handler; return generic 'Failed to search venues' message with 500 status

observability_requirements.note: Include correlation ID if available from request context

risks.description: Fuzzy matching too lenient (false positives): 'abc' matches 'abcdef' at 0.79 threshold
risks.mitigation: Threshold = 0.80; test boundary case: 'abc' vs 'abcd' should NOT match (similarity = 0.75); 'abc' vs 'abce' should NOT match

risks.description: Fuzzy matching too strict: legitimate 2-char typos not matched
risks.mitigation: Test with real venue names (e.g., 'Schleyer-Halle'); tune threshold if needed based on data
risks.acceptance_criteria: Typo test cases cover 1-2 char variations; manual QA validates real-world scenarios

risks.description: Performance regression: Levenshtein calculation takes > 20ms for 100 venues
risks.mitigation: Benchmark before/after; if > 20ms, optimize: (a) move to PostgreSQL levenshtein() for large datasets, (b) add client-side debounce (already 300ms)
risks.acceptance_criteria: Performance benchmark test passes; total orchestration < 200ms

risks.description: Similarity calculation crashes on edge case (empty string, very long string > 1000 chars)
risks.mitigation: Add bounds checks in calculateSimilarity; test with extreme inputs; test error handling in try/catch
risks.acceptance_criteria: No unhandled exceptions in calculateSimilarity tests; all edge cases return valid values

risks.description: Database query slow with large venue count (> 10k venues); trigram index not used
risks.mitigation: Verify trigram index is active: SELECT * FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='concert' AND indexname LIKE '%trgm%'; if missing, create it (migration exists)
risks.acceptance_criteria: Production database has trigram index; query time verified < 50ms

risks.mitigation: Log similarity scores at DEBUG level (invisible to users); document threshold choice in code comments
risks.acceptance_criteria: DEBUG logs include similarity score for false negatives; code comment explains 0.80 choice

risks.description: Timeout cascade: all 3 sources fail, users see 'No venues found' with no indication why
risks.mitigation: Never let all sources fail silently; log each timeout as WARNING; if all fail, log as ERROR with 'all sources unavailable'
risks.acceptance_criteria: Test case test_searchVenuesEnhanced_all_sources_timeout passes; error logged; graceful fallback to empty array

risks.description: Rate limiter edge case: clock skew or rapid requests at window boundary
risks.mitigation: Test rate limiter at window boundaries (59s, 60s, 61s); verify reset works correctly
risks.acceptance_criteria: Rate limit tests pass; 30 requests succeed, 31st fails, after 60s new request succeeds

risks.description: Missing test coverage: new functions have < 80% coverage, errors not tested
risks.mitigation: Implement 15+ test cases covering happy path, errors, edge cases; run coverage report; must show 80%+ on new code
risks.acceptance_criteria: Coverage report shows 80%+ line + branch coverage; no critical error paths left untested
